The '''63rd British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 21 February 2010 at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2009 in film|2009]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2009.

The nominees were announced on 21 January 2010.<ref>{{cite web |title=British Academy Film Awards in 2010 – Long List |url=http://static.bafta.org/files/long-list-0910-400.pdf |website=bafta.org |publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts |access-date=25 January 2026 |ref=Longlist |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100215053543/http://static.bafta.org:80/files/long-list-0910-400.pdf |archive-date=15 February 2010}}</ref><ref name="Nominations">{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2010/jan/21/bafta-nominations-list|title=Bafta nominations list 2010|date=21 January 2010|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref> Three films received the most nominations in eight categories; ''[[Avatar (2009 film)|Avatar]]'', ''[[An Education]]'' and ''[[The Hurt Locker]]''. ''[[District 9]]'' followed with seven.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/awards/film/film-awards-nominations,949,BA.html |title=2010 BAFTA nominees and winners |work=British Academy of Film and Television Arts |access-date=21 February 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100323165515/http://www.bafta.org/awards/film/film-awards-nominations%2C949%2CBA.html |archive-date=2010-03-23 |url-status=dead}}</ref> ''[[The Hurt Locker]]'' took home the most awards with six. This year marked the first year of the new Academy President, [[William, Prince of Wales]], who presented the [[BAFTA Fellowship]] award to [[Vanessa Redgrave]], alongside [[Uma Thurman]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/film-awards-in-2010-winners-announced|title=Film Awards in 2010: Winners Announced|date=21 February 2010|work=[[BAFTA]]|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref>

[[Jonathan Ross]] hosted the ceremony for the fourth consecutive year.
